{{Quote|Mira is the best, isn't she!?! Have you ever seen her dance? She was one of the top entertainers in Coronet until her brother died. Now she is here, and we are very lucky to have her.|[[O'choa Detera]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:OD">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=conversation|O'choa Detera|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
'''Mira T'rani''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[entertainer]] who lived during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|I know just the person: Mira T'rani! She could use a great Entertainer like you. She's in the cantina.|[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:YNW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|You Need Work|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>}}
Mira T'rani resided for a while on the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] with her brother. During that time, she became one of the top entertainers in [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet]]. However, after the death of her brother, she left the planet and moved to [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]], in the [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|Bright Jewel system]]. By the time following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Mira had become a renowned entertainer on Tansarii Point Station.<ref name="SWG:OD">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=conversation|O'choa Detera|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> She was working in the station's cantina and was a friend of [[Anvar Keyis]], the manager of the cantina.<ref name="SWG:StAK">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Entertainer|Speak to Anvar Keyis|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> a spacer arrived on Tansarii Point Station along with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. Since the spacer was herself an entertainer, Solo suggested her to meet Mira T'rani.<ref name="SWG:ET" /> The Twi'lek was glad to meet the spacer and proposed her to work for Anvar Keyis.<ref name="SWG:StAK" /> The later was very pleased to greet the spacer since he really needed new entertainers for the cantina. Due to troubles on Tansarii Point Station, few people were coming to the cantina and Keyis wanted to change this situation. The spacer performed as a dancer and was very successful, bringing many customers to the cantina.<ref name="SWG:BCttC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Entertainer|Bring Customers to the Cantina|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> After working for some time for Anvar Keyis, the spacer returned to Mira in order to perform other kinds of work.<ref name="SWG:RtM">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Entertainer|Return to Mira|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> Once, Mira requested the spacer to play music in the medical center of the station to entertain the wounded there.<ref name="SWG:EtW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Entertainer|Entertain the Wounded|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref> Another time, T'rani tasked the spacer to dance for the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] commanding the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] detachment stationed on Tansarii Point Station. In fact, the woman was very interested about the Imperial presence on the station, a very uncommon situation. She was convinced that good entertainers performing their art could obtain any information from anybody, what she named the "power of an Entertainer". The spacer was indeed a skilled entertainer who effectively managed to inspire the Imperial Officer and convinced him to reveal information about his mission on the station, namely retrieve and escort a stolen high-value artifact back to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="SWG:DfI">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=class|Entertainer|Dancing for Information|Tansarii Point Station}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Mira T'rani appeared as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:ET" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Mira T'rani was added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:New Player Tutorial|New Player Tutorial]],"<ref name="SWG:ET" /> introduced with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref>
